patient_information.diagnosis.answer: Status post left anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) reconstruction with hamstring autograft; associated left knee pain, stiffness, and quadriceps weakness

patient_information.precautions.answer: Post-op ACL reconstruction precautions: avoid open-chain resisted knee extension 0–30 degrees for first 6 weeks; no running or jumping until cleared by surgeon; brace locked in extension for ambulation until adequate quadriceps control; avoid pivoting/twisting on left limb; monitor for effusion and signs of DVT.

plan_of_care__treatment_orders.post_oper.answer: Left ACL reconstruction with hamstring autograft (follow standard post-operative knee rehabilitation protocol, Phase II–III progression as tolerated).

plan_of_care__treatment_orders.other.answer: May progress to jogging on level surface around week 12 post-op if: no effusion, full extension, flexion ≥125°, pain ≤2/10 with functional activities, and good single-leg control. Emphasize return-to-sport testing (Y-balance, hop tests, strength symmetry ≥90%) prior to clearance for competitive basketball or soccer. Communicate progress to surgeon at 6-week and 12-week intervals.

special_instructions.special_instruction.answer: Focus on restoring full knee extension early, gradual flexion gains, and progressive closed-chain strengthening with emphasis on quadriceps and hip abductors; prioritize neuromuscular control and proper landing mechanics.

Avoid aggressive open-chain knee extension early in rehab; use knee brace per protocol during community ambulation until quadriceps strength and control are adequate. Contact my office if pain, swelling, or instability significantly increase or if patient reports mechanical locking or catching.
